title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects differ among cities of varying sizes and climatic regions?
narrative: Relevant documents must describe the causes, measurement methods, and mitigation strategies of urban heat islands, offering comparative analysis across small, medium, and large cities in diverse climate zones, and include quantitative or case‑study evidence.

title: Carbon Capture Funding
description: What public financing mechanisms are available for large‑scale carbon capture and storage projects in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on governmental, state, and private funding programs that support large‑scale CCS initiatives. Relevant documents should describe eligibility criteria, application procedures, funding amounts, and recent case studies of projects that received support. Materials that only provide general climate change background or discuss CCS technology without reference to financing mechanisms are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentive Policies
description: What federal, state, and local policy mechanisms have been used to encourage residential solar panel installation in the United States from 2015 to 2023?
narrative: The user is researching the range of incentives—such as tax credits, rebates, net metering rules, and financing programs—that have been implemented to boost residential solar adoption in the U.S. during the specified period. Relevant documents should describe the type of incentive, the jurisdiction that enacted it, the timeframe of its applicability, and any measurable impact on solar installation rates. Exclude documents that discuss commercial solar, non‑U.S. policies, or general information about solar technology without policy context.

title: AI Safety Regulation
description: What are the current regulatory frameworks governing the safety testing of advanced AI systems in the United States?
narrative: The user wants authoritative, up‑to‑date information on federal, state, and industry guidelines, legislation, and standards that address safety testing, risk assessment, and compliance for advanced artificial intelligence models. Relevant documents should describe official regulations, pending bills, agency guidance, and credible analyses from government bodies, policy institutes, or recognized experts; informal blog posts, opinion pieces, or speculative commentary are not considered relevant.

title: Fitness Tracker Privacy
description: What personal data do modern fitness trackers collect, and how is that data stored, shared, or sold?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the types of biometric and contextual data harvested by contemporary fitness wearables, the technical and policy mechanisms governing its storage (local device, cloud services), and any third‑party sharing or commercial sale practices. Relevant documents should describe data categories, retention periods, encryption or anonymization measures, and privacy policies or regulations affecting these practices. Documents focusing solely on device hardware, user guides, or marketing claims without addressing data handling are not relevant.

title: Solar Grid Integration
description: What are the main technical, regulatory, and economic challenges of integrating large‑scale solar power into Europe’s existing electricity grids, and which policy mechanisms address them?
narrative: The user seeks in‑depth information—such as academic papers, government reports, or industry analyses—that discuss how high‑penetration solar photovoltaics affect grid stability, transmission capacity, market design, and investment in storage or demand‑response in European countries. Relevant content should describe at least one technical hurdle (e.g., intermittency, voltage control), one regulatory or policy instrument (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, capacity markets), and offer concrete examples or case studies from 2010 onward. Materials that only mention solar power in passing or focus solely on non‑European contexts are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the rise of remote work since 2020 affected traffic congestion patterns in major metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is looking for empirical studies, transportation reports, or scholarly analyses that quantify changes in traffic volume, peak‑hour congestion, and commuting distances attributable to increased remote work. Relevant documents should provide data before and after 2020, focus on large cities, and discuss methodological approaches or policy implications.

title: Electric Scooter Impacts
description: What are the documented environmental, health, and traffic safety impacts of electric scooters in major U.S. cities since 2018?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (2018‑present) research, policy analyses, and credible news reports that evaluate how electric scooters affect air quality, noise pollution, rider and pedestrian safety, and overall traffic flow in large metropolitan areas of the United States. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, comparative studies, or systematic reviews; opinion pieces, marketing material, or unrelated transportation topics (e.g., bike lanes, public transit without scooter context) are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Retention
description: How does remote work impact employee retention rates across different industries?
narrative: The user is looking for empirical evidence, analyses, or comprehensive reports that examine the effect of remote‑work policies on employee turnover. Relevant material should provide quantitative or qualitative data on retention metrics, compare outcomes across sectors such as technology, finance, healthcare, and consider variables like job level, employee age, tenure, and the length of remote‑work arrangements. Sources that only mention remote work in passing, present purely anecdotal opinions, or discuss retention without linking it to remote‑work practices are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Investment
description: What are the key factors to consider when evaluating renewable energy projects for investment in emerging markets?
narrative: The user wants detailed guidance on assessing renewable energy projects for investment, focusing on financial metrics, regulatory environment, technology performance, environmental impact, and market risk in emerging economies. Relevant material should cover risk assessment frameworks, return‑on‑investment analysis, government incentives, case studies of successful projects, and comparative technology evaluations. Content that only discusses renewable energy in developed markets, pure technical research without investment context, or unrelated financial topics is not relevant.

title: Remote Work Burnout
description: How does prolonged remote work affect burnout levels among mid‑career software engineers compared to on‑site workers?
narrative: The user is seeking empirical evidence—such as peer‑reviewed studies, surveys, or meta‑analyses—examining burnout among software engineers with 5 to 15 years of experience who have been working remotely for at least one year, and comparing those results to engineers in traditional office settings. Relevant documents should report quantitative burnout measures (e.g., Maslach Burnout Inventory scores) and may also include qualitative findings about stressors and coping strategies. Preference is given to sources published after 2015, preferably in English, and to research that controls for variables such as company size, project type, and work‑life balance policies.

title: Solar Incentive Policy
description: What federal, state, and local incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on monetary and regulatory support for homeowners wanting to install solar photovoltaic systems, including tax credits, rebates, net‑metering rules, and financing programs. Relevant documents must explicitly list or describe these incentives, specify the jurisdiction (federal, state, or municipal), and indicate the time frame (e.g., policies in effect for 2024‑2025). Sources that only discuss solar technology, market trends, or non‑residential incentives are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What key policy measures have European countries implemented since 2015 to promote renewable energy adoption?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive details on governmental policies, incentives, regulations, and targets aimed at increasing renewable energy use in Europe from 2015 onward. Relevant documents should describe specific legislation, subsidy programs, feed‑in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, and reported outcomes. Sources focusing on non‑European regions or periods outside the specified timeframe are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Impact
description: How has the increase in remote work since 2020 altered traffic congestion patterns in major U.S. metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is seeking recent empirical research, governmental or transportation agency reports, and credible news analyses that quantify changes in vehicle counts, travel times, and peak‑hour congestion attributable to remote‑work trends. Relevant documents should provide data comparisons before and after 2020, discuss methodological approaches, and focus on U.S. cities such as New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and Dallas.

title: EU Carbon Regulations
description: What recent changes have been made to the European Union's carbon trading system and how do they impact emissions reporting for heavy‑industry companies?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on EU carbon market reforms introduced after 2022, including the rationale behind the changes, specific rules affecting heavy‑industry sectors (e.g., steel, cement, chemicals), and the expected consequences for emissions reporting and compliance. Relevant materials should contain policy documents, regulatory analyses, industry impact assessments, or expert commentary that directly address these recent regulatory updates.

title: Remote Work Health
description: What are the psychological effects of remote work on employees and how can organizations mitigate negative outcomes?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed studies, industry reports, or well‑documented case analyses—that examine how remote work influences employee mental health. Relevant material should discuss stress, burnout, isolation, productivity changes, and related well‑being metrics, and it should offer evidence‑based strategies or best‑practice recommendations for employers to support their remote workforce.

title: Solar Incentive Policies
description: What are the most recent European Union policies and financial incentives that support residential solar photovoltaic installations?
narrative: The user is seeking up-to-date information on EU-level legislation, subsidy programs, tax credits, and other financial mechanisms that encourage homeowners to install solar PV systems. Relevant documents should discuss specific policy measures adopted after 2022, detail eligibility criteria, describe the scope of incentives (e.g., grant amounts, feed‑in tariffs, tax deductions), and compare implementation across member states. Materials that only mention general renewable energy goals, unrelated media streaming services, or non‑EU policies are not relevant.

title: EU Renewable Policy
description: What recent EU policy measures are intended to increase renewable energy adoption by 2030?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, authoritative information about European Union legislation, targets, financial incentives, and implementation timelines that aim to boost the share of renewable energy in the EU energy mix by the end of the decade. Relevant documents should cover official policy texts, regulatory frameworks, and analyses of expected impact, while excluding unrelated topics such as generic climate discussions or non‑EU initiatives.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: What strategies have major U.S. cities implemented to mitigate urban heat island effects?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed descriptions of policies, engineering measures, and community programs that large U.S. metropolitan areas (e.g., New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, Houston) have adopted to reduce temperature differentials caused by the urban heat island phenomenon. Relevant content should include specific actions such as green roofs, tree‑planting initiatives, cool‑pavement projects, zoning changes, and any evaluation of their effectiveness or impact on local temperatures.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What government incentives are available for homeowners to install solar panels in France in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks current, detailed information on national, regional, and local financial incentives, tax credits, and subsidy programs that support residential solar photovoltaic installations in France during 2024. Relevant material must describe eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary amounts or tax reductions offered, and any relevant deadlines or program expiration dates. Sources that only discuss electricity tariffs, supplier choices, or general energy market information without addressing solar-specific incentives are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key factors influencing the adoption of renewable energy policies in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on determinants that have shaped EU renewable energy policy adoption after 2010, including legislative frameworks, economic incentives, political dynamics, public opinion, and technological readiness. Relevant documents should provide analysis or empirical data on these factors, focus on EU member countries, and cover the period from 2010 to the present, preferably from scholarly articles, policy reports, or reputable think‑tank publications.

title: Carbon Pricing Impacts
description: What are the economic and environmental effects of implementing carbon pricing mechanisms in emerging economies?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed analyses of carbon pricing (taxes, cap‑and‑trade, or hybrid schemes) applied in emerging markets, emphasizing quantifiable outcomes such as GDP growth, emissions reductions, sectoral employment changes, and equity considerations. Relevant sources should provide case studies, empirical evaluations, policy design discussions, and documented unintended consequences specific to developing or transitioning economies. Materials that focus solely on developed nations, on renewable technology without price mechanisms, or that offer only generic climate‑change background are not relevant.

title: AI Ethics Standards
description: What are the key components of emerging global standards for ethical artificial intelligence deployment?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on recently developed (post‑2018) frameworks, guidelines, and policy documents that define ethical AI standards worldwide. Relevant material should describe core principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and safety, as well as governance structures, compliance mechanisms, and evaluation metrics. Sources may include governmental policy papers, international organization reports, academic surveys, and industry consortium guidelines that outline how these standards are to be implemented and monitored.

title: Remote Work Real Estate
description: How has the rise of remote work affected demand for office space in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is looking for recent analyses, statistics, and expert commentary that explain how remote work trends since 2020 have changed office vacancy rates, lease prices, and corporate relocation decisions in major metropolitan areas of the United States. Relevant material should include quantitative data, market reports, or case studies that directly link remote‑work adoption to shifts in office real‑estate demand.

title: Solar Farm Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives exist for developing utility‑scale solar farms in the United States?
narrative: The user is researching financial and regulatory support mechanisms for large‑scale solar energy projects. Relevant documents should describe federal tax credits (e.g., Investment Tax Credit), state‑level rebate or grant programs, financing options, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and any recent policy changes. Materials focused solely on small‑scale residential solar, unrelated renewable technologies, or non‑U.S. jurisdictions are not relevant.

title: Climate Migration
description: How are low‑lying coastal communities adapting to rising sea levels and what policies are being implemented to facilitate migration?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on adaptation and relocation efforts for populations displaced by sea‑level rise. Relevant materials include scholarly articles, government reports, and case studies that describe concrete strategies, policy frameworks, funding mechanisms, or evaluated outcomes for coastal communities facing migration pressures. Documents that merely discuss climate change in abstract terms, or that address migration without a focus on sea‑level rise, are not considered relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban design strategies mitigate the formation and impact of heat islands in densely populated cities?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy-oriented information on approaches such as green roofs, reflective materials, increased tree canopy, and zoning regulations that reduce temperature differentials between urban cores and surrounding rural areas. Relevant documents should discuss the effectiveness, implementation challenges, case studies, and measurable outcomes of these mitigation tactics.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What recent federal and state incentives exist for residential solar power installation in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on policies that financially encourage homeowners to install solar panels. Relevant documents should describe federal tax credits (e.g., Investment Tax Credit), state‑level rebate programs, net‑metering rules, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and any upcoming changes or deadlines. Sources that merely mention solar technology without policy details, or that focus on commercial/utility‑scale projects, are not relevant.

title: AI Ethics Guidelines
description: What are the most widely accepted ethical guidelines for developing artificial intelligence systems?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of established ethical principles, frameworks, and best practices used by academia, industry, and policymakers for AI development. Relevant material should cover concepts such as fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and societal impact, and reference recognized organizations or standards (e.g., EU AI Act, IEEE Ethically Aligned Design, OECD AI Principles).

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the projected economic and environmental impacts of achieving a 100% renewable electricity grid in the United States by 2050?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive analyses that discuss both the macro‑economic consequences (e.g., job creation, GDP effects, electricity pricing, investment requirements) and the environmental outcomes (e.g., CO₂ emissions reductions, air quality improvements, land and water use) of a nationwide transition to 100% renewable electricity by 2050. Relevant documents should contain quantitative forecasts, scenario modeling, policy recommendations, and assessments of feasibility, covering the U.S. context and citing credible sources such as government reports, peer‑reviewed studies, or reputable think‑tank publications.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What are the current federal and state tax incentives for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date information on financial incentives available to homeowners for installing solar PV systems, including the federal Investment Tax Credit percentage, any state‑level rebates or tax credits, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and relevant deadlines or phase‑out schedules.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects vary across different city morphologies and what mitigation strategies have proven most effective?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy-oriented information that explains the relationship between city layout (e.g., building density, green space distribution, surface materials) and the intensity of urban heat islands, and that evaluates mitigation measures such as reflective surfaces, urban forestry, and cool roofs. Relevant documents should discuss quantitative measurements, case studies from multiple climates, and evidence of effectiveness, while excluding generic weather reports or unrelated climate change discussions.

title: Eco Fashion Supply
description: How do major fashion brands ensure sustainability in their supply chains?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on the approaches that leading apparel companies use to make their supply chains more sustainable. Relevant documents should describe corporate policies, certification programs (e.g., GOTS, Fair Trade), measurable sustainability metrics, third‑party audit processes, and documented challenges or successes. Preference is given to sources from the past five years that include case studies, industry reports, or peer‑reviewed analyses of supply‑chain transparency and environmental impact.

title: Climate Migration 2050
description: What are the expected global migration patterns caused by climate change by the year 2050?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed articles, UN or IPCC reports, and reputable NGOs—that provide quantitative projections of people displaced or migrating due to climate‑related factors (e.g., sea‑level rise, extreme weather events, desertification) through 2050. Relevant documents should include estimates of numbers, regional breakdowns, timelines, and discuss potential policy responses or adaptation strategies. Content unrelated to climate‑driven human movement, such as commercial retail promotions or unrelated product listings, is not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal tax credits and state rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date information on financial incentives that homeowners can claim when installing solar photovoltaic systems, including the amount of the federal investment tax credit, any state‑level rebates or grants, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and recent legislative updates. Relevant documents should detail tax credit percentages, qualifying costs, deadlines, and policy changes. Documents about personality types, psychology, or unrelated subjects are not relevant.

title: Global Climate Records
description: Where can I find comprehensive, peer-reviewed global temperature and precipitation datasets covering the period 1900‑2020?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative, downloadable climate data sets that provide monthly or annual global surface temperature and precipitation measurements from 1900 to 2020. Relevant sources include major meteorological or research agencies (e.g., NOAA, NASA, Hadley Centre, ECMWF) that offer data in standard formats such as CSV or NetCDF and include clear documentation of methods and provenance. Documents that only discuss climate change trends, present regional or short‑term data, lack scientific validation, or do not provide raw data for the specified period are not considered relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What current federal and state tax incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date information on monetary incentives that homeowners can claim for installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant content must describe the federal Investment Tax Credit (percentage, phase‑out schedule), state‑specific tax credits, rebates, grants, eligibility criteria, application processes, and deadline dates. Documents focusing solely on commercial or utility‑scale solar, on unrelated renewable technologies, or that cite outdated incentive amounts are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal incentives are available for small‑scale solar installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for current, authoritative information on U.S. federal programs that support residential or small‑business solar photovoltaic projects, including tax credits, rebates, grants, and loan guarantees. Relevant material should detail eligibility requirements, the monetary value or percentage of the credit, application procedures, deadlines, and any recent legislative changes affecting these programs. Sources that only discuss state‑level incentives without linking them to federal policy, or that address solar technologies unrelated to PV installations, are not relevant.

title: Solar Policy Impact
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at accelerating solar power adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—academic papers, government reports, and reputable NGO analyses—that examine how specific policies (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, tax incentives, net‑metering, financing schemes) influence the deployment of solar photovoltaics in low‑income nations. Relevant documents should describe the policy design, implementation challenges, quantitative outcomes (e.g., installed capacity growth, cost reductions), and include case studies from 2010 onward. Content that only discusses solar technology without reference to policy, focuses exclusively on high‑income countries, or lacks empirical evidence of impact should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What are the most effective strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in mid‑size temperate cities?
narrative: The user seeks documents that discuss practical, evidence‑based measures to mitigate urban heat islands (UHI) in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000 located in temperate climate zones. Relevant material should include case studies, quantitative evaluations of effectiveness (e.g., temperature reduction, energy savings), cost considerations, and implementation guidelines for interventions such as green roofs, reflective pavements, urban forestry, cool walls, and district cooling systems. Sources that focus on tropical or arid cities, purely theoretical models without empirical validation, or strategies unrelated to temperature mitigation (e.g., traffic management) are not relevant.

title: Broadband Pricing
description: What are the current price plans, speeds, and contract terms for high‑speed internet services offered by major French ISPs?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed, side‑by‑side comparison of broadband offerings from providers such as Orange, SFR, Free, Bouygues Telecom, and Numericable. Relevant documents must include specific plan names, monthly fees, advertised download/upload speeds, data caps (if any), contract length, early‑termination penalties, promotional discounts, and information about bundled TV or mobile services. Only sources that provide concrete pricing and contractual details for the latest fiber or 5G broadband packages are considered relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date information on financial incentives that help homeowners afford solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant material must describe current federal tax credits (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), state‑specific rebate or grant programs, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and any recent policy changes (2022‑2024). Documents that only address commercial installations, non‑U.S. programs, or outdated incentives prior to 2020 are not relevant.

title: Solar Grid Integration
description: What are the main challenges and proposed solutions for integrating distributed solar power into national electricity grids?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on technical, regulatory, and economic obstacles to large‑scale adoption of distributed solar photovoltaic systems, as well as concrete mitigation strategies that have been suggested or implemented in the past five years. Relevant content should discuss grid stability, forecasting accuracy, energy storage, market mechanisms, and policy frameworks that enable higher solar penetration, and should cite specific case studies, pilot projects, or scholarly analyses. General overviews of solar technology that do not address grid integration issues are not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax incentives are available for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user is seeking up-to-date information on U.S. federal tax credits, rebates, or other financial incentives that apply specifically to residential solar panel installations for the calendar year 2024. Relevant documents must describe the eligibility criteria, the amount or percentage of the credit, filing procedures, and any recent legislative changes affecting the program. Sources that only discuss commercial solar incentives, state‑level programs, or outdated (pre‑2023) policies are not relevant.

title: Grid Storage
description: What are the most promising battery technologies for large‑scale grid energy storage and their projected costs and performance metrics by 2030?
narrative: The user is researching future large‑scale energy storage solutions to support renewable integration. Relevant documents should discuss specific battery chemistries (e.g., lithium‑iron‑phosphate, sodium‑sulfur, flow batteries, solid‑state, metal‑air), evaluate their technical performance (energy density, cycle life, efficiency, safety), and provide credible cost forecasts or market analyses for deployment at the grid level up to the year 2030. Sources that focus on policy, pilot projects, or comparative techno‑economic studies that meet these criteria are considered relevant, while documents about small‑scale consumer batteries, unrelated financial bonuses, or non‑technical descriptions are not.
